info.name: report-service
info.description:
Manual smoke + fixture collection for the report-service ingestion + read surface.

Ingestion endpoints require the `apiKey` header (env var `{{apiKey}}`). Health endpoints are anonymous.

Imports cleanly into Postman, Bruno (`Import → Postman Collection`), Insomnia, and Hoppscotch — the v2.1.0 Postman Collection format is the open de-facto interchange format.

Edit the `report-service · local` environment to point at a different host / apiKey when targeting staging.

exec: // Default assertion every request inherits: response is non-5xx and arrived in <5s.

item.name: Health
item.description: Anonymous liveness + readiness probes. The container's healthcheck hits `/healthz`; the API surface offers richer `/api/health` and `/api/health/ready`.

item.name: Ingestion — JSON crash reports
item.description: JSON ingestion endpoint (kind=crash). One POST per crash; the body is the canonical `RSCProblemReport` envelope. `stackTrace` is the only field the report-detail page renders verbatim.

body.raw:
{
  "platform": "android",
  "kind": "crash",
  "title": "java.lang.RuntimeException",
  "message": "Automatic crash report\nThread: main\nReason: UNCAUGHT_EXCEPTION\nSDK related: false\nMessage: Postman fixture crash",
  "deviceModel": "{{deviceModel}}",
  "appVersion": "{{appVersion}}",
  "source": "SDK",
  "pharmacyId": "{{pharmacyId}}",
  "userId": "{{installUserId}}",
  "labels": ["SDKV2", "fixture", "postman"],
  "stackTrace": "java.lang.RuntimeException: Postman fixture crash\n\tat de.example.fixture.PostmanCrasherKt.crashNow(PostmanCrasherKt.kt:42)\n\tat de.example.fixture.MainActivity.onCreate(MainActivity.kt:17)\n\tat android.app.Activity.performCreate(Activity.java:8595)",
  "occurredAt": "2026-05-12T10:00:00Z"
}

{
  "platform": "ios",
  "kind": "crash",
  "title": "DemoTriggeredCrash.postman_fixture: Postman fixture crash",
  "message": "Postman iOS fixture",
  "deviceModel": "iPhone",
  "appVersion": "0.0.1",
  "source": "demo-app",
  "labels": ["SDKV2", "crash", "fixture"],
  "userId": "{{installUserId}}",
  "stackTrace": "DemoTriggeredCrash.postman_fixture: Postman fixture crash\n0   IASDK                postmanFixtureCrash()\n1   DemoCrashHook        emit(trigger:message:)",
  "occurredAt": "2026-05-12T10:01:00Z"
}

{
  "platform": "android",
  "kind": "crash",
  "title": "java.lang.RuntimeException",
  "message": "Automatic crash report\nThread: main\nReason: UNCAUGHT_EXCEPTION\nSDK related: false\nMessage: Host app simulated crash from FlutterDemo MainActivity",
  "deviceModel": "{{deviceModel}}",
  "appVersion": "1.0.0 (SDK 2.3.41)",
  "source": "SDK",
  "pharmacyId": "",
  "userId": "{{installUserId}}",
  "labels": ["SDKV2", "1.0.0 (SDK 2.3.41)", "6001"],
  "stackTrace": "java.lang.RuntimeException: Host app simulated crash from FlutterDemo MainActivity\n\tat com.example.appsdk_v2_flutter_plugin_example.MainActivity$configureFlutterEngine$1.onMethodCall(MainActivity.kt:23)",
  "occurredAt": "2026-05-12T10:02:00Z"
}

item.name: Ingestion — Analytics events
item.description: kind=analytics events flow through the same `/api/v1/reports` endpoint. They show up on `/Analytics`, not `/Errors`.

body.raw:
{
  "platform": "android",
  "kind": "analytics",
  "title": "screen_view",
  "message": "User landed on the pharmacy detail screen",
  "deviceModel": "{{deviceModel}}",
  "appVersion": "{{appVersion}}",
  "source": "SDK",
  "pharmacyId": "{{pharmacyId}}",
  "userId": "{{installUserId}}",
  "labels": ["SDKV2", "analytics", "screen_view"],
  "eventProperties": {
    "screen": "PharmacyDetail",
    "referrer": "Apofinder"
  },
  "occurredAt": "2026-05-12T10:03:00Z"
}

item.name: Ingestion — Multipart Report a Problem
item.description:
The partner endpoint the IA SDK calls. Multipart form-data with `json` part (required) + optional `file` gzip attachment. Crash uploads and user-initiated RaP submissions both flow through here.

For uploads with a file: edit the `file` form field's `src` to a real local path. For the simple Postman-only smoke (no file), the JSON-only path on /api/v1/reports is easier.

formdata.value: {"platform":"android","title":"App is slow on the cart screen","message":"Loading the cart takes >10s on my phone after the last update","deviceModel":"{{deviceModel}}","appVersion":"{{appVersion}}","source":"SDK","pharmacyId":"{{pharmacyId}}","userId":"{{installUserId}}","email":"reporter@example.org","phone":"+49 1234 567890","labels":["SDKV2","RaP","manual"]}

item.name: Crash upload with gzip log (file required)
item.description: Replace the `file` field's `src` with a local `.log.gz` path (e.g. the one Android SDK's CrashReportUploader writes to the cache dir).

formdata.value:
{"platform":"android","kind":"crash","title":"java.lang.RuntimeException","message":"Automatic crash report
Thread: main
Reason: UNCAUGHT_EXCEPTION
SDK related: true
Message: Postman multipart fixture","deviceModel":"{{deviceModel}}","appVersion":"{{appVersion}}","source":"SDK","pharmacyId":"{{pharmacyId}}","userId":"{{installUserId}}","labels":["SDKV2","crash","fixture"]}

item.name: Reports browsing
item.description: Read-side: list stored reports per platform + fetch individual reports by file name. The admin UI calls the same endpoints internally.

item.name: Download a report JSON
item.description: Uses `lastFileName` set by the 'Android JVM crash' test script. Run that request first to populate it.

item.name: Forced reports
item.description: Mobile clients call `/api/v1/forced-reports/{id}` on every backend fetch with their stable identifier. The admin operator adds ids through `/ForcedReports`; this endpoint returns `forced=true` for those ids and the SDK then submits a forced RaP.
